sections.title: A: Gap Fill (Autonomous Delivery Drones Text)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: The autonomous delivery drone (1) ...... first trialled commercially in remote rural areas in 2016.
It is a compact unmanned aircraft that runs (2) ...... rechargeable lithium batteries and can navigate to its destination without any human input.
Flight stability (3) ...... maintained by an array of onboard sensors, which monitor the drone's (4) ...... movement in real time.
Whenever the aircraft is pushed off course by a gust of wind, the control system adjusts the rotors to compensate, (5) ...... this way keeping it on the correct flight path.
If the drone detects an obstacle in its path, the navigation software reroutes (6) ...... automatically to avoid a collision.
Once you are (7) ...... the tracking app, each delivery (8) ...... be followed in real time from the warehouse to the customer's door.
(9) ...... are thought to be a number of significant advantages to drone delivery for isolated communities and hard-to-reach areas.
Several major logistics companies, including courier firms and online retailers, consider (10) ...... to be a practical alternative to traditional road-based transport.

sections.title: B: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: The veteran chef stepped away from television in 2010, but he's now staging a ...................... with a brand-new restaurant in the city centre. **COME**
The patient had just woken from surgery and was still a little ...................... on her feet when the nurse helped her to walk. **STEADY**
The documentary about refugee families was one of the most ...................... films I have ever watched. **MOVE**
At the far end of the corridor, there was a concealed ...................... that connected the two wings of the old manor house. **PASS**
The new administration has outlined several ...................... policies aimed at reducing inequality and expanding access to healthcare. **PROGRESS**
Years of prolonged drought and economic mismanagement have ...................... the entire region. **STABLE**
Ramirez went on to break the national record, her earlier injury ...................... . **STAND**
She was pulled over by a traffic officer and handed her third ...................... fine of the year. **SPEED**

sections.title: C: Multiple Choice (Circle the correct word)

items.sentence: Could you **make / run / do** the children a bath before you put them to bed tonight?
She decided not to mention the possible promotion to her son, as she didn't want to **lift / raise / boost** his hopes unnecessarily.
The size of the screen is beside the **point / subject / theme** – the key question is whether the software actually works.
When the gallery offered her a solo exhibition, she **moved / jumped / ran** at the chance.
The professor's talk was fascinating, but I found her line of reasoning quite difficult to **track / follow / watch**.
It seemed like an impossible challenge at first, but eventually everything **fell / went / came** into place.
We were sitting outside enjoying the evening air when the temperature began to **go / turn / get** a little cold, so we moved inside.
Thousands of elderly people across the country will **go / get / become** without heating this winter, and that is deeply concerning.

sections.title: D: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
Torres passed the ball to Reyes just seconds before the final whistle blew. **By**
Reyes ...... Torres just seconds before the final whistle blew.

Sources say that investigators have uncovered a number of irregularities since the audit began. **reported**
There ...... a number of irregularities since the audit began.

Try to keep up with the leading group throughout the opening stages of the race. **fall**
Try ...... the leading group throughout the opening stages of the race.

Emergency services have put barriers around the collapsed bridge to prevent people from approaching. **cordoned**
The collapsed bridge ...... emergency services.
items.answer: has been cordoned off by the

items.sentence:
A neighbour of ours repainted our kitchen for us last weekend. **had**
We ...... our kitchen last weekend.

The head teacher always likes to congratulate students personally on their exam results. **point**
The head teacher always ...... students personally on their exam results.

After a hesitant opening, the crowd were soon cheering at the performer's routines. **had**
After a hesitant opening, the performer ...... at their routines.

I just need to send this email and then I'll come and join you. **way**
I just need to ...... and then I'll come and join you.
items.answer: get this email out of the way
